MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Steven A. Kronenberg of HAKE LAW PC (415-926-5800), appearing for defendant / moving party Bragg Investment Company, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Margaret Patrick, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BRAGG INVESTMENT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Defendant Bragg Investment Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patrick was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, even if Patrick Farley's deposition testimony is admissible, defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Farley does not have the knowledge identified in plaintiffs' interrogatory responses. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs cannot prove that asbestos exposure contributed to or caused Mr. Patrick's alleged asbestos-related illness and death. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
